assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines available versions as 'Versions you can manually upgrade to' and explains they are 'Manual upgrade options'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines default as 'Version used for new cluster creation' and explains it can differ from auto-upgrade target, though it doesn't specifically mention minor version introduction scenarios

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response defines auto-upgrade target as 'Version your existing cluster will upgrade to' and explains it's determined by cluster-specific factors like maintenance windows and constraints

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explains the difference between default (1.31.8) and auto-upgrade target (1.31.7) and mentions they can differ due to rollout sequencing, though doesn't specifically mention minor version rollouts

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions maintenance windows and exclusions as factors determining auto-upgrade target and explains it's cluster-specific based on configuration

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response does not mention 'no minor' exclusion policies or explain how they affect auto-upgrade targets to be patch-only

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info command and explains it shows auto-upgrade target and timing

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not mention or reference GKE release schedule or release notes as resources for tracking version availability
